title: How One Mom With Brain Injury Fought CA Laws Denying 50K Kids School Access
guestBio: Amy Bohn is the President and Co-Founder of PERK (Protection for the Educational Rights of Kids), one of California's most prominent advocates for parental rights and medical freedom. After recovering from a severe brain injury in 2018, she built a grassroots movement of over 30,000 members that has successfully challenged restrictive legislation affecting children's access to education.
excerpt: Amy Bohn couldn't read aloud after her brain injury, but when she discovered 50,000 California kids were denied school access, she built a 30,000-member advocacy movement. Her story proves that one determined mom can challenge an entire system.
metaDescription: Amy Bohn overcame brain injury to lead PERK's fight for 50K California kids denied school access. A mom's journey from recovery to advocacy.
keyTakeaways: Recovery from personal challenges can fuel your capacity to fight for others who need a voice.
Organized parent advocacy can successfully defeat restrictive legislation even in hostile political environments.
Understanding the systematic nature of legislative strategies helps you recognize patterns before they impact your family.
Building community around shared values creates exponential impact beyond what any individual can achieve alone.
Speaking up for vulnerable populations often requires stepping outside your comfort zone and into unfamiliar territory.
Legislative changes in one state often serve as testing grounds for nationwide implementation.
Effective advocacy requires both emotional passion and strategic understanding of political processes.

pullQuotes.text: What started as one exhausted mom showing up at the Capitol became a movement.

timestamps.label: Amy's brain injury and recovery journey
Discovering the impact of SB 277 and SB 276
Building PERK from the ground up
The reality of 50,000 children denied education

content:
Sometimes life breaks you open in ways that prepare you for battles you never imagined fighting. For Amy Bohn, that breaking point came in 2018 with a severe brain injury that left her unable to read a page aloud or walk across a room without exhaustion. But what she discovered during her recovery would ignite a fire that no brain injury could extinguish.

In this powerful episode of The Manage Her, Amy shares how she transformed from a struggling recovery patient into the President and Co-Founder of PERK (Protection for the Educational Rights of Kids), leading one of California's most effective grassroots movements for parental rights and medical freedom. Her story isn't just about overcoming personal adversity - it's about what happens when a mom refuses to accept that 50,000 children should be denied access to education.

When Recovery Meets Purpose

Amy's journey into advocacy began during her darkest hours. While battling the cognitive and physical challenges of brain injury recovery, she stumbled upon information about California's SB 277 and SB 276 - legislation that was systematically removing medical exemptions and denying children access to schools, daycares, and colleges. The injustice was so glaring that even in her compromised state, she couldn't ignore it.

This is where Amy's story becomes a masterclass in how personal struggle can become the foundation for extraordinary leadership. Instead of using her recovery as a reason to stay small and focus inward, she channeled her healing energy into protecting families who had no voice in the political process. Her brain injury didn't disqualify her from the fight - it qualified her to understand what it means to be vulnerable and need an advocate.

Building a Movement From Ground Zero

What started as one exhausted mom showing up at the Capitol transformed into a sophisticated advocacy organization with over 30,000 members. Under Amy's leadership, PERK has generated more than 5 million emails, phone calls, and measurable actions directed at legislators. But perhaps most impressively, in 2022, her coalition defeated 8 out of 12 extreme bills targeting medical freedom - proving that organized parents can win even in the most challenging political environments.

Amy's approach reveals something crucial about effective leadership: you don't need to be the loudest voice in the room, but you do need to be the most persistent. She understood that sustainable change requires building systems and communities, not just individual heroics. By creating PERK, she multiplied her impact exponentially while providing other parents with the tools and support they needed to become advocates themselves.

The Shocking Reality Behind the Headlines

The statistics Amy shares in this episode should wake up every parent in America. Fifty thousand children in California right now cannot attend school, daycare, or college because of these legislative changes. These aren't abstract policy debates - they represent real families who have been systematically excluded from basic educational access.

What makes Amy's work even more compelling is her ability to explain the step-by-step legislative strategy that created this crisis. She breaks down how SB 277 and SB 276 didn't just happen overnight - they were part of a deliberate, decade-long approach to removing personal belief exemptions, religious freedom, and medical autonomy from California families. Even more concerning, this blueprint is now being replicated in states across the country.

The Intersection of Personal Rights and Community Responsibility

Amy's advocacy touches on fundamental questions about informed consent, parental rights, and the balance between individual choice and collective policies. She references the Nuremberg Code and explains the historical context of medical autonomy in ways that help parents understand what's at stake beyond any single issue.

Her work also illuminates the complex relationship between pharmaceutical liability protection, government mandates, and family decision-making. By explaining the 1986 Act and its implications, Amy helps parents understand the broader systemic issues that affect their ability to make informed healthcare decisions for their children.

Leadership Lessons From the Trenches

Amy's story offers profound insights for any woman who feels called to lead in challenging circumstances. She demonstrates that you don't need to be fully healed, completely prepared, or universally supported to begin making a difference. Sometimes the most authentic leadership emerges from our deepest struggles and most personal experiences with injustice.

Her success with PERK also illustrates the power of building community around shared values rather than trying to go it alone. By creating space for other parents to contribute their skills, resources, and passion, Amy transformed a personal mission into a sustainable movement that continues to grow and adapt.

A Warning and a Call to Action

Perhaps the most important message in this episode is Amy's warning to parents outside of California: what happened here is coming to your state. The legislative strategies, the systematic approach to removing exemptions, and the exclusion of families from the educational system aren't unique to California politics. They're part of a coordinated effort being implemented across the country.

Amy's story proves that ordinary parents can achieve extraordinary results when they're willing to educate themselves, organize strategically, and persist through setbacks. Her journey from brain injury patient to Capitol warrior demonstrates that our most difficult personal experiences often prepare us for our most important contributions to the world.

If you're a parent who has ever been told that bill will never pass or there's nothing you can do about it, Amy's story is your wake-up call. The question isn't whether these changes are coming to your community - it's whether you'll be ready to respond when they do. And Amy Bohn's example shows us that being ready doesn't require perfection, just determination and the courage to speak up for those who need an advocate.
